sentences.text: I. Medical Necessity Aetna considers autologous hematopoietic cell transplantation medically necessary for the treatment of adults (18 to 69 years of age) with rapidly progressive scleroderma (systemic sclerosis) at risk of organ failure when either of the following is met: A. Active interstitial lung disease (as determined by broncho-alveolar cell composition or ground- glass opacities on computed tomography of the chest) plus either a forced vital capacity (FVC) or a diffusing capacity of the lung for carbon monoxide (DLCO) of less than 70 % of the predicted value; or B. Previous scleroderma-related renal disease; and C. The member meets the transplanting institutions' selection criteria, or in the absence of such criteria, the member does not have any of the following exclusion criteria: 1.
sentences.label: CLAIM

sentences.text: Experimental, Investigational, or Unproven A. Aetna considers hematopoietic cell transplantation (autologous or allogeneic) experimental, investigational, or unproven for any of the following because the effectiveness of these approaches for these autoimmune diseases/conditions has not been established: 1.
sentences.label: CLAIM

sentences.text: Autoimmune diseases (not an all-inclusive list): Anti-phospholipid syndrome Autoimmune cytopenia (e.g., autoimmune hemolytic anemia, Evans syndrome, and idiopathic thrombocytopenic purpura) Autoimmune diseases-induced cirrhosis Autoimmune retinopathy Behcet's disease with myelodysplastic syndrome Celiac disease Chronic inflammatory demyelinating polyradiculopathy Cogan syndrome Crohn's disease Dermatomyositis Diabetes mellitus type I Inflammatory bowel disease Juvenile idiopathic arthritis and associated lung disease Juvenile rheumatoid arthritis Lupus nephritis Multiple sclerosis Myasthenia gravis Neuromyelitis optica / neuromyelitis optica spectrum disorder Pemphigus Polymyositis Rheumatoid arthritis Sporadic late-onset nemaline myopathy Stiff-person syndrome / Stiff-person spectrum disorder Systemic lupus erythematosus Systemic vasculitis Ulcerative colitis. 2.
sentences.label: CLAIM

sentences.text: B. Aetna considers the following interventions experimental, investigational, or unproven: The use of mesenchymal stem cell transplantation for the treatment of autoimmune diseases (including ankylosing spondylitis and Sjogren’s syndrome) and selected indications listed in policy statement II.B; Administration (intra-arterial, intra-thecal, and intravenous) of autologous mesenchymal stem cells for the treatment of multiple system atrophy; Autologous umbilical cord blood stem cells for the treatment of autism; HLA-haploidentical hematopoietic stem cell transplantation for chronic granulomatous disease Pluripotent stem cell-based retinal organoids for restoration of blindness Umbilical cord blood transplantation in children with neuronal ceroid lipofuscinosis.
sentences.label: CLAIM

sentences.label: CLAIM

sentences.text: Furst (2000) noted that stem cell transplantation for the treatment of systemic sclerosis is showing some apparent effectiveness, but its use is only in the pilot stages.
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: Cipriani et al (2015) stated that the presence of autoimmune diseases, including systemic sclerosis (SSc), suggest failure of the normal immune regulatory processes leading to activation and expansion of auto- reactive effector immune cells.
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: The therapeutic strategy involves the ablation of the aberrant self-reactive immune cells by chemotherapy and the regeneration of a new self-tolerant immune system formed by the transplanted stem cells.
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: The authors concluded that although the results are encouraging, nonetheless, the correct application of stem cell transplantation remains an area of active investigation; results of larger randomized, double- blind clinical trials, will certainly improve the knowledge of the appropriate clinical use of stem cell therapy in SSc patients.
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: The authors concluded that AHSCT is beneficial in some patients with SSc and that patient selection and conditioning regimens are critical determinants of prognosis and mortality post-ASCT.
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: The authors concluded that the findings of this study confirmed that the AHSCT was effective in prolonging survival, as well as in inducing a rapid reduction of skin involvement and disease activity, and preserving lung function in patients with rp-dcSSc.
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: The experts from the European League against Rheumatism (EULAR, 2017) recommended that hematopoietic stem cell transplantation (HSCT) should be considered for the treatment of selected patients with rapidly progressive SSc at risk of organ failure.
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: The authors concluded that in the absence of an effective pharmacotherapy for SSc, autologous HSCT has a huge potential in management of cutaneous and internal organ manifestations.
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: The authors concluded that myeloablative autologous hematopoietic stem-cell transplantation achieved long-term benefits in patients with scleroderma, including improved EFS and OS, at a cost of increased expected toxicity.
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: For now, RPE cells and photoreceptors derived from patient-specific iPS cells can serve as a valuable tool in elucidating the mechanism of pathogenesis and drug discovery for AMD.
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: The authors concluded that intra-spinal transplantation of human spinal cord-derived neural stem cells can be safely accomplished at high doses, including successive lumbar and cervical procedures.
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: This study provided Class IV evidence that for patients with ALS, spinal cord transplantation of human stem cells can be safely accomplished and did not accelerate the progression of the disease.
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: The authors concluded that this meta-analysis and systematic review suggested that HSCT could be effective in the treatment of refractory CIDP.
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: The authors concluded that these preliminary results showed that HDC followed by ASCT seems feasible and safe and might result in long- term improvement of patients with RCD type II whose condition did not respond promptly to available drugs.
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: The authors concluded that they identified loss of function mutations in IL-10 and IL-10R in patients with very early onset IBD.
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: The authors concluded that AHSCT was relatively safe and appeared to be effective in controlling otherwise treatment-resistant CD.
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: The authors concluded that AHSCT may be a promising therapeutic option for patients with refractory CD.
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: The authors noted that high-dose immunosuppression and AHST were performed with acceptable toxicity in a small number of patients with newly diagnosed type 1 DM.
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: The authors concluded that the findings of this meta-analysis revealed MSCs therapy may be a safe and effective intervention in subjects with diabetes; however, due to the limited studies, a number of high-quality as well as large-scale RCTs should be performed to confirm these conclusions.
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: The authors concluded that this systematic review highlighted the positive impacts observed from employing MSCs in patients with T1DM.
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: The authors concluded that non-myeloablative autologous hemopoietic stem cell transplantation in patients with relapsing-remitting MS reverses neurological deficits, but these results need to be confirmed in a randomized trial.
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: The author noted that a direct comparison of aHSCT with conventional treatments in multi-center randomized controlled trials and publication of 5 to 10 years long-term follow-up data from cohort studies and international registries are a necessary step in further defining the role of aHSCT in the management of MS.
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: The authors concluded that intense immunosuppression followed by AHSCT is significantly superior to MTX in reducing MRI activity in severe cases of MS.
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: The authors concluded that based on these data AHSCT appeared to be a reasonable option in selected patients with highly inflammatory MS; however a RCT is needed.
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: The authors concluded that available evidence suggested that some clinical benefits of HCT when combined with immunotherapy on MS.
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: The authors concluded that HDIT/HCT without maintenance therapy was effective for inducing long-term sustained remissions of active RRMS at 5 years.
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: The authors concluded that AHSCT using the BEAM-ATG scheme is safe and effectives to control the aggressive forms of RRMS.
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: The authors concluded that the emerging evidence on this therapeutic approach in MS indicated that the largest benefit/risk profile form this therapeutic approach can be obtained in patients with aggressive MS with a relapsing-remitting course and who have not yet accumulated a high level of disability.
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: The authors concluded that AHSCT may be a therapeutic option for children with disease that does not respond to standard care.
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: The authors concluded that these findings suggested that suppression of Th17 cytokines was essential for the induction of long-term remission in MS patients following AHSCT.
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: The authors concluded that the use of AHSCT in individuals with active SPMS was associated with a slowing of disability progression and a higher likelihood of disability improvement compared with standard immunotherapy.
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: The authors concluded that their 7-year follow-up results suggested that auto-SCT seemed beneficial for SLE patients.
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: Moreover, they stated that recruitment of more patients into multi-center, randomized, comparative studies versus conventional treatment of SLE is needed to evaluate the safety and effectiveness of auto-SCT.
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: Therefore, fetal-type MSCs, such as umbilical cord- derived MSCs, represent an excellent alternative source of MSCs.
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: The authors concluded that there are still many questions regarding the clinical application of MSCs to HSCT that need to be answered, and further studies are needed.
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: The authors concluded that preliminary results of HSCT as a therapeutic option for SLE appeared promising.
sentences.label: PREMISE
